content:
𝗗𝗶𝗱 𝗬𝗼𝘂 𝗞𝗻𝗼𝘄? 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗼𝗹𝗲.𝗹𝗼𝗴(), 𝗮𝗹𝗲𝗿𝘁(), 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘁() 𝗔𝗿𝗲𝗻’𝘁 𝗝𝗮𝘃𝗮𝗦𝗰𝗿𝗶𝗽𝘁!

2 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Surprised? These commonly used functions aren’t actually part of the JavaScript language itself. 😲
They come from 𝗪𝗲𝗯 𝗔𝗣𝗜𝘀 provided by the browser — not the core JavaScript engine.
That’s why in environments like 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲.𝗷𝘀, alert() and prompt() don’t exist, but console.log() does (because Node defines its own console).
Knowing the difference between JavaScript and browser APIs helps you write cleaner, environment-aware code.
Were you aware of this detail before?
Let’s discuss
